title: How Ant Colonies Decide Without a Boss
script_summary: Ants use simple local rules to solve complex problems without a leader.

segments.voiceover: What if I told you ants make decisions without a boss?
Ant colonies thrive on swarm intelligence, where each ant follows simple rules.
They communicate through pheromones, guiding decisions without a hierarchy.
In experiments, groups of ants find food based on local interactions, not orders.
Imagine solving complex problems by simply following your instincts!
This decentralized decision-making leads to smarter outcomes—no boss required.
Want to learn more about the wonders of nature? Like and follow for more!
